title: Wrath & Glory - An Abundance of Apocryphal Adversaries
description:
A bestiary module for the Wrath & Glory system (Cubicle 7), adapting the threats from An Abundance of Apocryphal Adversaries — Nathan Dowdell's homebrew bestiary companion to An Abundance of Apocrypha — converted here with his permission.

Drop ready-to-play enemy stat blocks straight onto your maps: rank-and-file infantry, walkers and war machines, psykers, C'Tan, and the galaxy's most notorious Warlords and Overlords, each with their full weapons, talents, abilities and threat-tier options.

Current content — nine factions complete:

  • Orks — 55 threats (from Shoota Boyz to Ghazghkull Mag Uruk Thraka, including the full Kommando specialists and Wrecka Krews from source v2) plus 19 Adversary Traits and 15 Shiny Gubbinz relics.
  • Necrons — 39 actors (warriors, Destroyers, Crypteks, Canoptek constructs, the three C'Tan Shards, every named Overlord and Lord from Imotekh to Trazyn, and the Annihilation Barge, Night Scythe and Monolith war machines) plus 38 Lords of the Necrontyr items: Adversary Traits, Cryptek Arcana, and Relics of the Aeons.
  • Tyranids — 28 threats (Hormagaunts and Termagants through Genestealers, Carnifex, the Hive Tyrant, Tervigon, Trygon, Mawloc and beyond) plus the 13 Powers of the Hive Mind, a full Bio-Weapons armoury, and the seven Hive Fleet adaptations.
  • Drukhari (source v3) — 54 actors: the Kabals (Kabalite Warriors, Archon & court, and the full Hand of the Archon retinue), Wych Cults (Wyches, Reavers, Hellions, Beastmasters & beasts, Succubus), Haemonculi Covens (Wracks, Grotesques, Cronos/Talos Pain Engines, Haemonculus), Incubi, Scourges and the Mandrakes with all their variants — eight named lords (Asdrubael Vect, Aurelia Malys, Baron Sathonyx, Drazhar, Duke Sliscus, Lelith Hesperax, Kheradruakh, Urien Rakarth) and three vehicles (Razorwing Jetfighter, Voidraven Bomber, the Dias of Destruction) — with 50 items: Kabal/Cult/Coven adaptations, Adversary Traits and Artefacts of Cruelty. Power Through Pain updated to the v3 Pain Token economy.
  • T’au Empire — 59 actors: Fire Caste infantry, the full drone range, Stealth/Crisis/Broadside suits and Commanders, the Ethereal/Earth/Air/Water castes, Kroot and Vespid auxiliaries, the Ghostkeel and Riptide, named heroes (Aun’Shi, Aun’Va, Farsight, Shadowsun, Darkstrider, Longstrike) and the seven grav-tanks/aircraft as vehicles — plus 50 items (Sept adaptations, Support Systems, Adversary Traits, Prototype & Signature Systems).
  • Asuryani (Craftworld Aeldari) — 45 actors: Guardians, Warlocks & Rangers, the full Aspect Warrior shrines (Dire Avengers, Fire Dragons, Striking Scorpions, Howling Banshees, Swooping Hawks, Warp Spiders, Dark Reapers, Shining Spears and the Crimson Hunter) with their Exarchs, the Autarch/Farseer/Spiritseer, the Wraith constructs (Wraithguard, Wraithblades, Wraithlord), the Avatar of Kaela Mensha Khaine, eleven named heroes (Eldrad Ulthran, the six Phoenix Lords, Illic Nightspear, Iyanna Arienal, Nuadhu Fireheart and Prince Yriel) and the Engines of Vaul aircraft as vehicles — plus 33 items (Craftworld adaptations, Adversary Traits and Remnants of Glory).
  • Daemonic Legions (Chaos Daemons) — 52 actors: Khorne’s blood legions (Bloodletters through Bloodthirster, Skarbrand and Doombreed), Nurgle’s plague hosts (Plaguebearers through the Great Unclean One, Rotigus and Epidemius), Slaanesh’s hedonists (Daemonettes through the Keeper of Secrets and Shalaxi Helbane), Tzeentch’s sorcerous flux (Pink/Blue/Brimstone Horrors through the Lord of Change and Kairos Fateweaver), and the Legions of the Warp (Daemon Prince of Chaos, Chaos Furies, Be’Lakor) — with daemonic chariots and mounts as vehicles — plus 86 items: the four psychic disciplines (Warprot, Soulstain, Pandaemoniac, Noctic) and every god’s Adversary Traits, Exalted Greater Daemons and Relics.
  • Astra Militarum (Imperial Guard) — 88 actors: core infantry of Cadia/Catachan/Krieg (and Conscripts), officers, commissars, veterans, the twelve Veteran specialists, Tank Crew/Commanders and Field Ordnance, Rough Riders with their Grox/Horse/Mukaali/Saurian mounts, the full Militarum Tempestus (Scions, Aquilons and Tempestor Prime), psykers (Wyrdvane & Primaris), Ogryns/Bullgryns/Ratlings, the Kasrkin kill team and Catachan Devils, sixteen named heroes (Marbo, Yarrick, Straken, Creed & Kell, Ursula Creed, both Lord Solars with mounts, Pask, Nork Deddog, Mogul Kamir and more) and three vehicles (Rogal Dorn, Salamander Command & Scout tanks) — plus 42 items: the Psykana psychic discipline, Hymns of Battle, Adversary Traits and the Heirlooms of Conquest.
  • Malicious Miscellany — 63 actors across four unrelated groups: the worm-mass Slaugth; the rat-like Hrud Skavengers (Verminkin and Stormvermin through the Eshin assassins, Moulder beasts, Skryre engineers, Pestilens plague-priests and the Pale Seer); the Seraphon (Skinks, Saurus, Kroxigor, Skink Starpriest, Saurus Oldblood and Slann Starmaster); and a large Agents of the Imperium block — an Adeptus Custodes Custodian Guard, the Sisters of Silence, the full Adeptus Arbites precinct, the Inquisition (Mystic to Inquisitor) and an Astropath, the Navis Imperialis Breacher kill team, and all four Officio Assassinorum temples (Callidus, Culexus, Eversor, Vindicare) — plus 27 items: the Warpvolt, Ruin, Celestial and Telethesia psychic disciplines.
  • Heretic Astartes (Chaos Space Marines) — base legion (v0.9.1): 71 actors — the full Legionary family, Chosen/Raptors/Warp Talons/Havocs/Obliterators/Mutilators/Possessed/Terminators, the Traitor Guard regiment, Fellgor Beastmen, Chaos Cultists/Hounds/Spawn, every HQ (Exalted Champion, Dark Apostle, Master of Executions, Warpsmith, Master of Possession, Chaos Sorcerers, Chaos Lords and the Daemon Prince), the daemon engines (Helbrute, Defiler, Forgefiend, Maulerfiend, Venomcrawler) and five named lords (Haarken, Abaddon the Despoiler, Huron Blackheart, Fabius Bile, Vashtorr) — plus 52 items: the Dark Hereticus, Malefic, Dark Commune and Fellgor psychic disciplines, the Chaos Prayers, Adversary Traits and Relics & Daemon Weapons.
  • — Death Guard (Nurgle) cult legion (v0.9.4): 17 actors — Plague Marines, Poxwalkers, Blightlord & Deathshroud Terminators, the specialists (Biologus Putrifier, Foul Blightspawn, Noxious Blightbringer, Plague Surgeon, Tallyman, Malignant Plaguecaster), the Lords of Contagion and Virulence, the daemon engines (Foetid Bloat-drone, Myphitic Blight-hauler, Plagueburst Crawler), Typhus and the Daemon Primarch Mortarion — plus 40 items: the Contagion psychic discipline, Adversary Traits (with the seven Contagions), Relics of Decay and Deadly Pathogens.

Still to come: the remaining Heretic Astartes cult legions (Emperor’s Children, Thousand Sons, World Eaters), then the work-in-progress Adeptus Astartes supplement once finalised.

⚠️ Recommended companions: wng-core (icons and the Smite power), wng-apocrypha (the Ork WAAAGH! and Aeldari Rune psychic powers), and wng-xenos (Necron, T’au and Aeldari tokens). The module installs and runs without them, but those icons, tokens and linked powers will be missing. Some mounted Ork bosses also reference Church of Steel (wng-cos) for their vehicles.

Warhammer 40,000 and Wrath & Glory are the IP of Games Workshop and Cubicle 7; this is an unofficial, non-commercial fan project.
